title: Quantum Multi-Level Estimation of Functionals of Discrete Distributions
authors: Kean Chen, Minbo Gao, Tongyang Li, Qisheng Wang, Xinzhao Wang
abstract: We propose a quantum multi-level estimation framework for a functional $\sum_{i=1}^n f(p_i)$ of a discrete distribution $(p_i)_{i=1}^n$. We partition the values $p_i$ into logarithmically many intervals whose length decays exponentially. For each interval, we perform non-destructive singular value discrimination to isolate the relevant $p_i$, enabling adaptive estimation of the partial sum over this interval. Unlike previous variable-time approaches, our method avoids high control overhead and requires only constant extra ancilla qubits. As an application, we present efficient quantum estimators for the $q$-Tsallis entropy of discrete distributions. Specifically: (i) For $q > 1$, we obtain a near-optimal quantum algorithm with query complexity $\tildeΘ(1/\varepsilon^{\max\{1/(2(q-1)), 1\}})$, improving the prior best $O(1/\varepsilon^{1+1/(q-1)})$ due to Liu and Wang (SODA 2025; IEEE Trans. Inf. Theory 2026). (ii) For $0 < q < 1$, we obtain a quantum algorithm with query complexity $\tilde{O}(n^{1/q-1/2}/\varepsilon^{1/q})$, exhibiting a quantum speedup over the near-optimal classical estimators due to Jiao, Venkat, Han, and Weissman (IEEE Trans. Inf. Theory 2017). Our results achieve, to our knowledge, the first near-optimal quantum estimators for parameterized $q$-entropy for non-integer $q$.
summary: This paper introduces a new quantum algorithm for estimating complex functions of probability distributions, such as various types of entropy. It achieves near-optimal efficiency for many cases while using much fewer extra qubits and simpler control logic than previous methods.
main_problem: Developing efficient quantum algorithms for estimating functionals of discrete probability distributions, particularly those with singularities like q-Tsallis and Shannon entropies.
main_result: The authors propose a multi-level estimation framework that achieves near-optimal quantum query complexity for q-Tsallis entropy for q > 1 and provides a quantum speedup for 0 < q < 1, while significantly reducing ancilla and control overhead.
method: A multi-level framework using logarithmic partitioning of the probability space, non-destructive singular value discrimination, and Quantum Singular Value Transformation (QSVT) for local polynomial approximation.
model_or_system: A purified quantum query access model where a unitary oracle provides access to the square roots of the probabilities of a discrete distribution.
key_observables: q-Tsallis entropy, Shannon entropy, alpha-Renyi entropy, and general functionals of the form sum(p_i * g(p_i)).
important_parameters: The parameter q (for Tsallis/Renyi entropy), the precision epsilon, the distribution size n, and the interval partitioning ratio rho.
main_assumptions: Requires purified quantum query access, the existence of suitable polynomial approximations for the target function, and that the matrix norm is bounded.
figures_summary: Table 1 compares the quantum query complexity of estimating q-Tsallis entropy across various ranges of q against previous classical and quantum results.
paper_structure: The paper introduces a multi-level estimation framework, details the technical implementation using non-destructive discrimination and QSVT, provides complexity analysis for various entropy regimes (Shannon, Tsallis, Renyi), and establishes matching lower bounds via sample-to-query lifting.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
